label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Today is September 9, 1909. I need to find the date exactly 24 hours later. Hmm, 24 hours later is the same as one full day later, right? So, adding one day to the current date. So if today is the 9th, adding one day would be the 10th. Sep has 30 days, I think. So September 10th is still within the same month. So the date should be September 10, 1909. The year doesn't change here because September has 30 days, so going from the 9th to the 10th is within the same month and same year. Looking at the options given, option A is 09/10/1909. That seems correct. Let me double-check. 24 hours is a day, so adding one day to 9/9 is 9/10. Yep, that makes sense.
